name: Turathi Blue
layering: Apply directly over {Toro} to frame an ultra-modern citrus above the mineral vetiver drydown.
review: Gold-standard Tygar clone. Piercing, realistic grapefruit that stays crisp throughout thanks to a massive dose of high-quality ambroxan.

name: Aariz
review: Smooth, slightly sweeter take on the Tygar DNA. Lacks the synthetic sting of cheaper freshies; clean linen and bright rind.

name: Majesty
review: Powdery, refined floral nuance from Orris/Jasmine, capturing the premium 'Vibrato' profile over a strong, clean base.

name: Rasasi Hawas
inspiration: High-Performance Fruity Aquatic (Original Rasasi DNA — not an Invictus clone)
heart: Watery Notes, Plum, Orange Blossom, Cardamom
review: Legendary synthetic summer powerhouse. Intense fresh cinnamon-spiced apple and heavy marine components; monstrous trail in heat. Has its own original DNA — not a direct Invictus Aqua clone.

name: Jacques Yves Imaginari
base: Chinese Black Tea, Ambroxan, Guaiac Wood
review: Uplifting hot-weather casual. Captures the luxury soap-meets-fizzy-ginger-tea profile of LV Imagination effortlessly.

name: Aquatica
inspiration: Creed Virgin Island Water (Creamy Lime Coconut)
base: Coconut Milk, Sugar Cane, White Rum, Musk
review: Dense, rich tropical execution favouring creamy coconut milk and zesty lime over an airy ozone structure. Solid density on hot skin.
layering: Top with {Marine Blue} to add a bright citrus-tiare sparkle over the creamy coconut base.

name: Toro Pour Homme Glace
top: Icy Citron, Bitter Grapefruit, Juniper Berries
base: Clean Woody Notes, Sharp Earthy Vetiver
layering: Apply directly over regular {Toro} to add a crisp, frosty citrus burst to the mineral drydown.
review: Crisp icy companion to regular Toro. Frosty grapefruit and juniper over mineral-vetiver base — lighter and colder than the original.

name: Cedrat Essence Emir
top: Sicilian Lemon, Blackcurrant, Bergamot, Spicy Notes
heart: Fruity Notes, Patchouli Leaf, Water Jasmine
review: Rich oriental take on Cedrat Boisé. Blackcurrant and patchouli deepen the lemon-citrus opening; leather and oakmoss anchor a warm vanilla base.
layering: Pair with {Qaed Al Fursan} for a massive fruity-woody pineapple boost.

name: Wander
inspiration: Louis Vuitton Meteore / Fresh Designer Hybrid
heart: Neroli, Tunisian Orange Blossom, Pink Pepper
review: Light, airy Mediterranean freshness. Clean neroli and orange blossom with a pink pepper lift over a soft patchouli base — easygoing warm-weather casual.

name: Jean Lowe Immortel
layering: Top with {Club De Nuit Intense Man} (3:2) for a crisp, smoky-ginger trail.
review: Balanced corporate signature. Crisp, tart ginger-grapefruit fusion mellowing into a sharp ambergris background. Highly professional.

name: L'infinite Jacques Yves
review: More linear and ambroxan-heavy than Jean Lowe Immortel. Budget office staple — ginger-grapefruit over a thick ambroxan amber base.

name: Parfum D'Hommes Sport
review: Clean, slightly metallic Dior Homme Sport replica. Elemi resin adds a resinous citrus edge over a woody frankincense base. Casual and unobtrusive.

name: Fusion Intense
review: Light coconut-citrus fusion with rosemary and geranium heart. Ambroxan provides a clean mineral base. Beach casual or light office wear.

name: Al Dirgham Limited Edition
review: Warm, sunny Allure Homme Sport adjacent. Creamy lemon and lemongrass opening into a soft floral heart; vanilla and tonka give a sweet, approachable drydown.

name: Island Dreams
review: Simple, pleasant LV Symphony-adjacent. Fresh ginger-bergamot with a grapefruit sparkle over clean ambroxan — light and easy casual wear.

name: Pinnace Oryn
review: Light Afternoon Swim clone. Mandarin-bergamot with a ginger sparkle over an ambergris whisper — casual warm-weather choice.
layering: Spray over {Oud Madness} to pull a fresh citrus burst over the base notes.

name: Shiyaaka Snow
review: SMW-adjacent icy freshness. Pink pepper and bergamot over a clean cardamom-vetiver base with a nutmeg spice note. Closer to SMW than a Reflection hybrid.

name: Night Club Silver
inspiration: Creed Silver Mountain Water (Metallic Violet Leaf & Citrus)
review: Silver Mountain Water inspired. Violet leaf and ginger give an inky green freshness; blackcurrant adds a tart metallic edge over a white musk-cedar base. Mountain-air aura without screech.
layering: Layer over {Divin Vert} for a metallic green-citrus lift above the earthy oakmoss chypre.
flag: ⚠️ AUDIT: Prior notes were Creed SMW's Fragrantica listing copied verbatim. Updated to actual clone notes from Fragrantica. Green Tea note not confirmed for this clone.

name: La Uno Forever Blue
review: Pleasant D&G Light Blue Forever riff. Ozonic violet leaf over a clean white musk-vetiver base — breezy and uncomplicated.

name: Daring Blue For Life
review: The weaker sibling to La Uno Forever Blue. Grapefruit rind and salt water fade quickly — use as a top-note refresher rather than a standalone wear.

name: Pacific Blue (Porto Neroli)
review: Clean, airy Neroli Portofino adjacent. Bitter orange and neroli over a jasmine heart with soft amber musk — bright Mediterranean freshness.

name: Missoni Wave
review: Clean sporty sea-salt freshness with a rosemary-clary sage backbone. Cedar and vetiver add a dry woody finish. Versatile casual wear.
layering: Layer over {Ajmal Evoke Gold for Him} for an upscale luxury barbershop soap profile.

name: Nautica Voyage
review: Timeless green aquatic classic. Crisp apple and lotus over clean oakmoss and cedar — the definitive budget freshie and a crowd-pleaser at any age.

name: Versace Pour Homme
review: Classy, understated Mediterranean signature. Crisp lemon-neroli over hyacinth and sage with a warm tonka-musk base — professional and inoffensive.

name: L'Eau d'Issey Pour Homme
review: Iconic aromatic yuzu classic. Bright citrus-sage opening with a nutmeg-lotus heart over Tahitian vetiver. A foundational scent that influenced a generation of freshies.

name: Aether Extrait
review: PdM Greenley adjacent with improving projection as the bottle matures. Clean lavender-violet heart over a clearwood-amberwood base — polished and professional.
layering: Overlay over {Montblanc Explorer} to create an exceptional professional clean sillage field.

name: Citadel
review: Polished BdC-adjacent woody aromatic. Dry cedar and incense with a grapefruit-peppermint opening and long vetiver drydown. Professional and versatile.

name: Club De Nuit Iconic
review: Armaf's premium BdC EDP interpretation. Nuttier and richer than CDN Intense Man — excellent office-to-date bridge with ginger-nutmeg complexity and long incense-vetiver drydown.
layering: Base layer under {Afnan Rare Carbon} for an incredible clean leather trail.

name: Maitre De Blue
review: Budget BdC EDT riff. Accurate opening that fades quickly — better used as a trigger layer under a stronger scent than worn standalone.

name: Yeah! Man
review: Reliable YSL Y alternative. Clean apple-ginger over sage and juniper with an amberwood-tonka base. Professional, safe, and crowd-pleasing.

name: Armaf Odyssey Mega
review: Versatile YSL Y twist with Armaf's characteristic tonka-cedar depth. Ginger-bergamot over juniper-sage — a step warmer and spicier than standard Y.

name: Sheikh Al Shuyukh Final Ed.
review: Beast-mode YSL Y Intense inspired. Heavy apple-sage core with extra patchouli and amber ballast — bolder and darker than standard Y clones.
layering: Pair with {Khamrah Qahwa} for a rich, warm gourmet apple-pie output.

name: Armaf Odyssey Aqua
review: Invictus Platinum inspired. Clean, airy mint and lavender over an ambroxan-patchouli base — professional freshness without the aquatic density.

name: Dumont Nitro Red
review: Clubbing powerhouse hybrid. Sweet watermelon-strawberry bomb over amber-vanilla base — polarising but undeniably loud. Best in cold weather clubbing contexts.

name: Armaf Odyssey Tyrant
review: Unique Tygar-Sauvage fusion. Ambroxan-driven with elemi citrus, geranium, and lavender over an amber-woody base. Beast performance with sophisticated office-to-evening versatility.

name: Coach Platinum
review: Clean professional scent with a pineapple-juniper lift. Cashmeran and sage heart gives a smooth aromatic character; vanilla and leather base adds warmth.

name: Narciso Rodriguez Bleu Noir
review: Sophisticated musky blue with excellent date-night appeal. Dense cardamom and nutmeg open into a musk-dominant heart over an ebony-cedar base — intimate and distinctive.

name: Versace Dylan Blue
base: Mineral Musk, Tonka Bean, Incense, Saffron
review: Reliable casual-to-office blue. Bergamot and fig leaf open into a violet-papyrus heart with a mineral musk-tonka-saffron base. Versatile and crowd-pleasing.

name: Platine Blanc
inspiration: Xerjoff Torino 21 (Ice-Cold Citrus, Tonic Water & Ambergris)
review: Ice-cold Torino 21 adjacent. Fizzy tonic water and crushed mint open over a blackcurrant-jasmine heart; sandalwood and oakmoss anchor the drydown. Understated office elegance.
layering: Apply over {Sovereign} for an icy tonic-mint top above the rich leather-sandalwood drydown.
flag: ⚠️ AUDIT: Prior data had wrong inspiration ('luxury soap') and fabricated Iris/Aldehyde notes. Corrected to Xerjoff Torino 21 clone — verify this is the correct product you ordered.

name: Qaed Al Fursan
review: Budget pineapple powerhouse. Non-smoky, hyper-juicy pineapple-saffron opening over balsam fir and cedar oud — an outstanding layering base that amplifies any dark woody foundation.
layering: Layer directly over {Afnan Rare Carbon} or {Lattafa Asad} to inject an intense, sweet fruit top note.

name: Shiyaaka for Men
top: Rosemary, Red Pepper Berries, Bitter Orange Leaves
review: Amouage Reflection Man alternative at a fraction of the price. Dense iris-jasmine floral heart over a rich patchouli-sandalwood base. Beast performance and a genuinely luxurious feel.

name: Supremacy Collector's Ed.
review: Afnan's premium tier, praised for richer oil concentrations that eliminate synthetic chemical openings.
layering: Layer heavily over {Lattafa Asad} (3:2 sprays) to compile a rich, modern footprint.
flag: Source files differ on exact name (Supremacy Series vs Collector's Ed.) — confirm which you own.

name: Club De Nuit Intense Man
review: The legendary entry-level Aventus clone. Smoky birch and pineapple with extraordinary beast-mode performance — arguably the best value-to-quality fragrance on the market.
layering: Overlay over {Liquid Brun} to buffer heavy smoke with dense gourmet vanilla.

name: Montblanc Explorer
review: Smooth, polished Aventus-adjacent. Pink pepper and sage over haitian vetiver-leather with an ambroxan-akigalawood base — office-safe, inoffensive, and well-executed.

name: Ajmal Evoke Gold for Him
review: Elegant Prada L'Homme-inspired. Black pepper and neroli over an iris-geranium heart with patchouli-cedar base — refined, professional, and understated.
layering: Base layer under {Missoni Wave} for an elegant laundry-style finish.

name: Dark Door Intense
review: Faithful Dior Homme Intense clone. Lavender over an iris-root-pear heart with virginia cedar and vetiver base — refined, formal, and polished without the designer price tag.

name: Night Club (Free Gift)
review: Well-balanced Aventus profile. Smooth transitions from juicy pineapple opening to dry, masculine smoky birch core. Free gift.

name: Confidential Platinum
review: Chanel Platinum Égoïste tribute. Classic barbershop rosemary-lavender backbone over clary sage and jasmine with an oakmoss-vetiver base. Timeless masculine elegance.

name: Maison Alhambra Toro
review: Faithful Terre d'Hermès clone. Dirty orange-grapefruit opening over a classic earthy vetiver and patchouli base. Solid office signature.
layering: Base layer under {Turathi Blue} to cleanly update traditional mineral accords.

name: Afnan Rare Carbon
review: Ombre Leather alternative with oud complexity. Smoky leather-violet-cinnamon opening with a rose-cedar heart over amber-sandalwood-vetiver. Dark and seductive.
layering: Base layer under {Club De Nuit Iconic} for a sharp citrus-leather fusion.

name: Supreme L'homme Extreme
review: Guerlain Idéal Extrême inspired. Almond-plum-cinnamon opening with a heliotrope heart over a tobacco-leather-patchouli base — dark, seductive, and well-suited to evening dates.

name: Montblanc Legend EDP
review: Modern leather fougère. Violet leaf and jasmine-magnolia over a clean leather-oakmoss base — refined and versatile for office or casual wear.

name: Montblanc Legend EDT
review: Classic aromatic fougère. Lavender-pineapple-red apple over oakmoss and tonka-sandalwood — a safe, office-friendly signature that almost anyone will like.

name: Cuba Gold
review: Budget JPG Le Male riff. Lavender-mint-cinnamon over a vanilla tonka base — classic fougère structure at a very accessible price point.

name: CK Euphoria Men
review: Spicy-fresh CK accent. Ginger-pepper over black basil and sage with an amber-suede-patchouli base. Fades fast but is well-structured for intimate evening wear.

name: Sui Generis
inspiration: Givenchy Gentleman Society Extreme (Coffee, Iris & Nutmeg)
review: Workspace winner. Coffee and orris give a clean, slightly roasted powdery character over a smooth palo santo vanilla base — more sophisticated than standard ambroxan freshies.
layering: Base layer under {Khamrah Qahwa} to deepen the roasted-coffee heart with candied dates and praline.
flag: ⚠️ AUDIT: Heart notes corrected — Coffee and Orris are the DNA markers of Gentleman Society Extreme. Prior notes (Haitian & Madagascar Vetiver) were wrong. Inspiration updated to Extreme variant (not standard GS).

name: Divin Vert
base: Oakmoss, Patchouli, Leather, Rum, Tobacco, Vetiver
review: Heavy, complex, masterfully balanced masculine chypre. Self-assured alpha aura: pineapple over bitter leather, dark tobacco, damp moss.

name: Armaf Checkmate
review: JPG Scandal Pour Homme clone done right. Rich caramel and roasted tonka over earthy vetiver — playful yet unmistakably masculine. Good cold-weather date or clubbing option.
layering: Overlay over {Afnan Rare Carbon} to wrap dark, dry leather inside a rich caramel cloud.

name: Liam Grey
base: Sandalwood, Vanilla Bourbon, Tonka Bean, Patchouli
review: BDK Gris Charnel inspired. Creamy fig and black tea over powdery iris-labdanum with bourbon vanilla and patchouli — sophisticated and intimate date-night elegance.
layering: Mingle with {Liquid Brun} for an exceptionally deep, sweet, spiced vanilla-tea sillage cloud.

name: Mauzoon
base: Agarwood (Oud), Sandalwood, White Musk, Patchouli, Amber
review: Rich Middle Eastern saffron-oud original. Peach and vanilla soften the saffron opening while oud and patchouli add depth in the base — warm, distinctive, and effortlessly luxurious.

name: Olfactory Bonfire
review: MM Replica By The Fireplace clone with beast performance. Chestnut and guaiac wood over cloves and juniper; vanilla and peru balsam give a warm, smoky-sweet amber finish.
layering: Layer behind {Khamrah Qahwa} to elevate the roasted coffee with smoky-chestnut embers.

name: Nuke 2.0
top: Red Habanero Chili Pepper, Red Fruit Compote, Saffron
base: Tobacco Leaf, Benzoin Resin, Warm Woods
review: Spicebomb Infrared beast at budget pricing. Red chili and cinnamon bomb with tobacco-benzoin drydown — loud, unapologetic, and made for cold-weather clubbing.

name: Mousuf
review: Unique chocolate-grape oud blend. Dark plum-grape with subtle chocolate over a guaiac wood sandalwood base — unexpectedly distinctive for casual cold-weather wear.
layering: Pair alongside {Pinnace Oryn} to contrast dark chocolate-grape notes with bright orange.

name: Royal Blend
review: Angels' Share inspired. Cognac-cinnamon-plum over a warm myrrh-iris heart with a vanilla-tonka-sandalwood base — opulent, boozy, and absolutely cold-weather date material.

name: Khamrah Qahwa
inspiration: Roasted Coffee / Kilian Angels' Share riff
heart: Roasted Coffee Beans, Candied Dates, Praline, White Florals
review: Addictive cold-weather stunner. Dark roasted coffee over the syrupy candied-date base of Khamrah, cutting back the baseline sweetness.
layering: Base layer under {Sheikh Al Shuyukh Final Ed.} for spiced bakery sillage.

name: Oud Madness
review: Dark, opulent oud-leather. Passionfruit and saffron open over a thick oud-patchouli heart with leather-amber-vanilla base — statement formal wear.
layering: Base layer under {Pinnace Oryn} to contrast a fresh orange burst against deep oud.

name: Glorious Oud Royal Blanc
review: Clean white musk interpretation. Magnolia and blackcurrant over a soft musk-mandarin heart with sandalwood and patchouli base — understated and versatile for office.

name: Zenith
review: Mind Games Queening inspired. Creamy vanilla-coconut with powdery iris and jasmine florals over a clean vanilla-musk-cedar base — intimate and comforting date-night warmth.

name: Harmony Code Absolute
review: Faithful Armani Code Absolu clone. Green mandarin and apple over orange blossom-nutmeg with a tonka-vanilla-suede base — reliable, attractive, and versatile for dates.

name: Ramz Silver
review: JPG Ultra Male alternate with a lighter touch. Pear-lavender-mint over cardamom-sage with a clean vanilla-amber base — slightly more refined than 9PM for evening casual.

name: Afnan 9 PM
review: King of high-performance loud clubbing scents. Sweet hyper-fruity bubblegum pear/apple opening into a warm, dense vanilla blanket.

name: Lattafa The Kingdom
review: Le Male Elixir inspired beast. Mint-honey-benzoin over madagascar vanilla with tobacco and honeyed woods — powerfully sweet and dark, purpose-built for cold-weather clubbing.

name: Detour Noir Exclusif
base: Guaiac Wood, Laotian Oud, Vanilla, Coffee
review: Layton Exclusif's near-twin at a fraction of the cost. Almond-vanilla opening over a complex guaiac-oud-coffee base — rich, formal, and beast-mode in performance.

name: Maison Alhambra Hercules
base: Vanilla, Iso E Super, Cedar, Musk
review: PdM Herod clone at a fraction of the price. Cinnamon-pepper open into a tobacco-incense heart; vanilla and Iso E Super give a warm, woody-cedar finish. Reliable date-night warmth.

name: Maison Alhambra Your Touch
review: Armani SWY inspired. Cardamom-pineapple opening into a warm chestnut-vanilla amberwood base — accessible, likeable, and well-executed for casual autumn/winter wear.

name: Victorioso Nero
review: Invictus Victory inspired. Pink pepper-olibanum over lavender with a vanilla-tonka amber base — darker and warmer than standard Invictus, works well in cold weather.

name: Lattafa Asad
review: Potent masculine dark spice bomb. Drops fresh Sauvage for a heavy, retro licorice, cardamom, and thick lavender layer. Spray carefully.

name: Liquid Brun
review: Elite buttery-smooth gourmand. Avoids the cheap 'candle-wax vanilla' trap via an airy guaiac wood backbone and warm cinnamon flakes.
layering: Mingle with {Khamrah Qahwa} or {Olfactory Bonfire} to add roasted-coffee or smoky-chestnut depth to the buttery bourbon vanilla.

name: La Uno Million Le Parfum
review: 1 Million Parfum inspired. Solar-pine opening over monoi and tuberose heart; suede-leather-labdanum base gives it depth. Warmer and richer than standard 1 Million.

name: Aqua Oud
review: Unique fresh-marine oud original. Blue sky accord and marine elements soften the Cambodian oud into an office-appropriate warmth — distinctive and unlike standard aquatics.

name: Lattafa Khamrah
base: Vanilla, Tonka Bean, Benzoin, Amberwood, Myrrh
review: Angels' Share golden clone. Cognac-cinnamon opening over a praline-date heart with a benzoin-myrrh backbone. Warmer and slightly deeper than Khamrah Qahwa.

name: Glacier Le Noir
inspiration: JPG Le Male Le Parfum (Iris, Cardamom & Vanilla)
review: Intimate evening / cooler date companion. Powdery cardamom opening into a creamy, dark, highly comforting vanilla.

name: Rifaaqat
inspiration: YSL Babycat (Suede Leather & Dark Incense Vanilla)
review: Cloning masterpiece. Non-sweet dark vanilla pod with raw premium suede leather and smoky incense trails. Smells opulent and high-end. Nuclear performance.
layering: Base layer under {Pinnace Oryn} or {Club De Nuit Iconic} to lift the dark suede-incense with a bright citrus opening.

name: Raees Luxury Edition 180ml
review: Value masterstroke. 180ml jumbo bottle replicating ADG Profumo: incense-forward marine with a calone-bergamot opening over a clean patchouli-cedar base.

name: Marine Blue
inspiration: Louis Vuitton City of Stars (Powdery Tiare Flower Citrus)
top: Blood Orange, Lemon, Mandarin Orange, Lime
review: LV City of Stars clone praised as 'incredibly good' for DNA accuracy. Opens with a bright citrus burst; tiare flower and jasmine take over with a powdery, beachy quality — reviewers describe it as smelling like you spent the day at the beach, layered sunscreen, and rinsed off in clean water. Floral and slightly powdery throughout, unisex-leaning feminine. Tiare flower is the loudest element. Minimal sprays needed for 6+ hours of projection.
layering: Layer over {Aquatica} to stack powdery tiare florals atop creamy coconut-lime for a full tropical-beach accord.

name: Versus Ocean Bleu
top: Water Notes, Calabrian Bergamot, Grapefruit, Fig Leaf
heart: Ambroxan, Papyrus, Black Pepper, Violet Leaf, Patchouli
review: Fragrance World's Dylan Blue clone with extra Sauvage ambroxan-papyrus depth layered in. Bergamot-grapefruit-fig leaf opening over an ambroxan-violet-patchouli heart; saffron and incense warm the drydown in a way the original doesn't. Community lands at 80–85% similarity to Dylan Blue. The saffron-incense base gives it an almost 90s-style warmth. Best-value entry into the Dylan Blue DNA.
layering: Base layer under {Marine Blue} to float bright citrus-tiare florals over the warm saffron-incense drydown.
flag: ⚠️ AUDIT: Longevity and projection unverified — update after first wear.

name: Arsh Extrait
inspiration: Guerlain L'Homme Idéal Platine Privé (Citrus Almond Neroli)
review: L'Homme Idéal Platine Privé clone that outperforms the original in both longevity and projection. Grapefruit-bergamot opens cleanly before soapy-fresh neroli and smooth creamy almond come forward. Creamy cedar-vanilla drydown matches Platine Privé's signature finish closely. One reviewer flags a faint dusty note in the very late drydown. Described as the warm-weather cousin to Prada L'Homme Intense — sophisticated, clean, and versatile.
layering: Spray over {Enigma} to lift the resinous geranium-balsam base with soapy neroli and creamy almond.

name: Winsome
inspiration: YSL Tuxedo (Violet Leaf, Rose & Amber Floral)
heart: Lily of the Valley, Rose, Black Pepper
review: Maison Alhambra's YSL Tuxedo clone (formerly sold as 'The Tux'). Opening reads 1:1 — violet leaf, coriander, and bergamot into a rose-lily of the valley heart. After 30 mins the base amber-vanilla settles in a way that lacks the original's depth. Community divided: some love it as a faithful budget Tuxedo; others flag a cloying resinous vanilla in the late drydown. Performance is the known weakness, fading to a skin scent by the 4th–5th hour.
layering: Anchor over {Afnan Rare Carbon} to give the short-lived violet-rose a leather-amber backbone that extends the wear.
flag: ⚠️ AUDIT: Longevity and drydown quality vary by batch — update after first wear.

name: Rave Now
inspiration: Juicy Pineapple Bomb (Aventus Layering Tool — Original)
review: Hyper-juicy pineapple bomb built for Aventus layering — not a direct Creed clone but solves the pineapple gap every Aventus clone has. Community loves it over CDN Intense Man or any Aventus base to inject a realistic bright pineapple top. Wearable solo in summer as a fun casual freshie. Considered a no-brainer at the price.
layering: Layer over {Club De Nuit Intense Man} or {Afnan Supremacy Not Only Intense} to inject a realistic pineapple top note.

name: Pour Homme
top: Lavender, Lemon, Bergamot, Clary Sage, Tarragon, Neroli, Mandarin Orange, Orange
heart: Pepper, Cinnamon, Coriander, Geranium, Violet, Jasmine, Rose
base: Tobacco, Tonka Bean, Sandalwood, Musk, Cedar, Amber, Coumarin, Iris
review: D&G's original 1994 aromatic fougère — a genuine classic, not a clone. Opens with an energising citrus-lavender burst (bergamot, mandarin, orange, tarragon) before a spiced floral heart of pepper, cinnamon, and rose develops. The tobacco-tonka-sandalwood base is where it earns its reputation: rounded, smooth, and soft yet unmistakably masculine. Lasts and projects all day, versatile enough for any season or occasion. A staple that still holds up 30 years on.

name: Hectic
inspiration: Louis Vuitton Imagination (Sparkling Citrus & Ginger Tea)
review: Bujairami's LV Imagination clone — citrus-forward with a brighter, punchier orange and ginger than the original. Black tea and guaiac wood anchor the drydown. Bujairami reviewers report long-lasting performance and more compliments than LV Imagination itself.
layering: Compare directly against {Jacques Yves Imaginari} to find your preferred Imagination DNA.

name: Sovereign
inspiration: Bleu de Chanel L'Exclusif (Rich Woody Amber Sandalwood)
review: Bujairami's take on BdC L'Exclusif — fresh sweet citrus and herbal mint open into a ginger-jasmine-nutmeg heart, drying down to a rich leather-resin-sandalwood base. Stays firmly in the BdC family but with an especially strong, dense drydown that outlasts the original. Reviewers report nine-hour wear with excellent quality for the price.
layering: Base layer under {Platine Blanc} to ground the icy tonic-citrus with a leather-resin foundation.

name: Enigma
review: Bujairami's Prada Paradigme clone built on an inverted-pyramid architecture — warm benzoin-balsam base pushed forward through a geranium-rose heart. Resinous, balsamic, easy-wearing. The Paradigme original divides community (generic vs well-complimented daily) but Bujairami pricing removes the risk.
layering: Top with {Arsh Extrait} for a bright citrus-almond opening over the warm benzoin-balsam drydown.

name: Too Damn Good
inspiration: Byredo Bal d'Afrique Absolu (Citrus Floral Woody — Extrait)
review: Bujairami's Extrait-level Byredo Bal d'Afrique Absolu clone. African marigold and citrus over a soft floral-jasmine heart with vetiver-cedar-ambrette drydown. Byredo original is notorious for weak performance — the Extrait concentration should address this.

name: Revival
top: Ginger Rind, Cardamom, Lavender, Black Pepper, Mahogany
base: Dark Incense, Tobacco Leaf, Vanilla, Vetiver, Leather
review: Bujairami's interpretation of the long-discontinued Gucci Envy for Men. Community is split — satisfied buyers report it's 90% accurate with good longevity; purists are harsher on construction quality. Ginger-cardamom-lavender opening over a sandalwood-carnation heart with a dark incense-vetiver-leather base. Ordered at EOFY50 price — update longevity after first wear.
flag: ⚠️ AUDIT: Longevity and projection unverified — update after first wear.

name: Hawas Verde
top: Crushed Mint Leaves, Key Lime, Calabrian Lemon
review: Ultimate scorching-day antidote. Near-identical Torino 21 clone — fizzy ice-cold mojito and botanical mint over a light patchouli-vetiver base. Hyper-refreshing.

name: Pacific Rock Moss (clone target)
review: Therapeutic coastal profile — mineral, salty sea breeze on damp rocks and green moss. Avoids heavy synthetic blue chemicals.

name: Mahd Al Dhahab
inspiration: Born To Stand Out — Drunk Lovers
top: Spilled Cognac, Red Berries, Pink Pepper
review: Praised Drunk Lovers alternative. The opening is brighter and fresher than the original; the drydown smooths into a softer, slightly sweeter boozy vanilla. Community consensus: blending is actually better than BTSO, and it projects longer on some wearers. The catch is batch dependency — performance varies widely, with some bottles being quiet. At a fraction of the Born To Stand Out price, the drydown quality is a consensus win.

name: Love Again / Cedar Horizon
top: Solar Accords, Green Galbanum, Crisp Ginger
review: The LV LVERS DNA is warm and solar — galbanum-ginger brightness over creamy sandalwood and cedarwood. Community describes a light, skin-close softness like a beach morning; not a loud freshie. Longevity is the known weakness of both clone candidates. Fragrantica notes on the original confirm a laid-back, casual-weekend signature that doesn't overstay its welcome.
flag: Two candidate clones listed — pick one when purchasing.

name: Astrodome
inspiration: Gucci Guilty Pour Homme Parfum (Lavender Nutmeg Labdanum)
review: Hamidi's Gucci Guilty Pour Homme Parfum clone. Lavender and nutmeg are the dominant notes; projects noticeably for the first hour then largely disappears. EDP format, unisex. Reviews note the DNA match is accurate but performance is the persistent weakness — spray heavily and don't expect it to last a full day.
flag: ⚠️ AUDIT: Performance widely reported as weak — verify longevity on your skin.

name: Ginger + Mist (Original)
base: Akigalawood, Gin Accord, Georgywood, Musk, Sandalwood
review: 2025 Michael Malul original by nose Yann Vasnier. Celebrated on Fragrantica as one of the best ginger scents ever released — sparkling frosted ginger-ale brightness (bergamot, pineapple, cedar) opening into a coriander-nutmeg heart, drying down into akigalawood and sandalwood. Longevity splits the community (1 hour for some, 12+ as a skin scent for others). Kept here as the reference DNA baseline for clones.
flag: This is an original (the thing being cloned), not a clone — kept here as a reference baseline.

name: Antique
inspiration: BDK Gris Charnel-adjacent (Creamy Sandalwood, Fig & Cardamom)
top: Fig, Black Tea, Cardamom, Milk, Coconut
base: Sandalwood, Tonka Bean, Amber, Musk, Caramel
review: Community strongly compares it to BDK Gris Charnel — same creamy, sophisticated oriental territory but with more vanilla-coconut warmth and a prominent fig-black tea opening vs Gris Charnel's smoother tea-iris structure. Reviewers call it 'wonderfully unisex', warm and intimate. Needs maceration to fully open — one month reportedly transforms both intensity and blending. Great value for the Niche Emarati tier.

name: Voux Elegante
base: Tobacco Leaf, Vanilla Pod, Tonka Bean
review: Gets approximately 75% of the way to Xerjoff Naxos — the honey-tobacco backbone is recognisable but the opening reads soapier and less refined than the original. Closes the gap significantly after 10–15 minutes of drydown as the cashmeran and tonka settle. Fragrantica reviewers note it's 'good value considering it costs 1/10th the price of Naxos'. Longevity is skin-dependent: 4–5 hours on some wearers. Strong formal evening candidate at an accessible price.

name: Eau Santal / Saffron 4
review: Dry papyrus-iris Santal 33 interpretation. Community confirms a recognisable santal opening but longevity is the recurring concern — some get a strong first hour before the drydown becomes flat and fades fast. The Montagne version is generally regarded as the more faithful and longer-lasting of the two options; Maison Alhambra's Saffron 4 opens closer but fades faster on some skin types. Pick Montagne if longevity is the priority.
flag: Dual clone option listed — pick one.

name: Suqraat
inspiration: Giorgio Armani Acqua di Gio Profumo
review: Community consensus: 90–95% Acqua di Gio Profumo at around $18 — widely called the best budget Profumo dupe available. The incense drydown is divisive: some love it as a natural-smelling surprise, others find it heavier and sweatier than the original's cool aquatic transparency. Full-day longevity widely confirmed. The only real gap vs the original is ADG Profumo's clean mineral transparency — Suqraat runs a touch denser and warmer in the base.

name: Caballo Pour Homme
review: Gent's Scents spotlighted this as a genuine hybrid of Bvlgari Tygar and Bleu de Chanel at ~$32. Grapefruit-lemon-ginger opening with an ambrette-violet sparkle over a thick ambroxan-patchouli base. Fragrantica reviewers confirm 8+ hour longevity. One reviewer called it 'better than Turathi Blue outright.' Blind-buy safe: wearable anywhere, broadly appealing, solid performance.

name: Italiano Nero
heart: Praline, Cinnamon, Black Cardamom, Tolu Balsam
base: Patchouli, Black Amber, Ebony Wood, Rosewood
review: Community is sharply divided: half say it's 'EXACTLY Black XS on first spray', the other half say it has 'nothing to do with Black XS.' The praline-cinnamon-cardamom DNA is the point of alignment; the gap is in construction refinement and batch consistency. Performance is the wildcard — some report under an hour, others get 6+ hours. Worth a sample before buying; at its price point the risk is low, but manage expectations on projection consistency.

name: Daiman Eclipse
review: Smoother, more approachable ADG Profumo alternative from Zimaya (Afnan's prestige line). Less incense-forward than the original — violet-amber heart and a clean patchouli-labdanum drydown.

name: Forbidden Love
base: Vanilla, Tonka Bean, Cinnamon, Benzoin, Clove, Sandalwood, Patchouli, Vetiver, Cedar
review: Big cherry bomb with a marzipan edge — an unabashed Lost Cherry dupe the community broadly agrees gets 80–85% of the way there at a fraction of the price. Opens explosively sweet with black cherry and bitter almond, sweetens into a plummy rose core, and settles into a warm vanillic base with dark spice. Immense projection early on; some find the base spice note jarring, others love the complexity it adds. Formerly sold as Lovely Cherie. Consensus: a crowd-pleaser on the right skin.

name: Ra'ed Absolu
review: 2026 Lattafa clone of Ralph Lauren Polo 67 (P67). Opens with Golden Delicious apple and mandarin, moving into an orange-flower, jasmine, and ylang-ylang heart, closing on a soft vanilla-musk base with a slightly mossy oakmoss finish. Community confirms the P67-inspired DNA — same addictive, modern, crowd-pleasing vibe. Fruity-floral and versatile; moderate longevity (~6 hours). Over-delivers at the price. Note: reviewers say the sibling Ra'ed Intensely has the smoother drydown of the two.

name: Ra'ed Intensely
review: 2026 Lattafa Prada Paradigme clone — widely praised as one of the closest budget Paradigme dupes, with reviewers saying it 'equals the Paradigme drydown.' Fresh grapefruit-yuzu-bergamot citrus opening dries down to a smooth sandalwood-ambroxan base with a clean vanillic warmth. Community consensus: significantly better drydown and performance than its sibling Ra'ed Absolu, 6-10 hour longevity, high-end Paradigme vibe at a fraction of the price. An easy office/daily win.

name: Naif
review: A warm, spiced aromatic that bridges the bakery and the wardrobe — cardamom-spiked bergamot over plum, into a lavender-artemisia-davana heart, closing on tonka and labdanum. Wearers enjoy the cosy, crowd-friendly sweetness. The major caveat: longevity is genuinely poor, sometimes under 90 minutes on dry skin. Community tip is to layer over unscented body lotion to extend wear meaningfully. Low price keeps the risk manageable — but spray heavily and set expectations.

name: Throne Eclipse
review: Maison Asrar's acclaimed spicy-sweet, rated 8.4/10 by the community. Cardamom and clove lead with an apple freshness on top; cinnamon and geranium warm the heart; amber and vanilla close with depth. Sophisticated rather than shouty — the spice is refined, not raw. Gets consistent compliments in cooler months. Community calls it a cosy fall/winter daily driver that occasionally crosses into date-night territory. Strong value proposition.

name: Regent
base: Musk, Cedarwood, Sandalwood, Ambergris, Patchouli, Tonka
review: 2026 Maison Asrar release — a bright citrus-led fresh with grapefruit-ginger-bergamot opening into an orange blossom and orris heart, settling on musk-cedarwood-sandalwood. Community loves the DNA and freshness but longevity is the known pain point: some report barely 2–3 hours on first use. Community tip is to let the bottle rest for maceration after opening. At its price point the citrus freshness is lovely, but be prepared to top up.

name: Vanguard
top: Pineapple, Blackcurrant, Grapefruit, Pink Pepper, Apple, Bergamot
heart: Cardamom, Lily of the Valley, Ginger, Carnation
base: Musk, Cashmere Musk, Ambergris, Leather, Cedarwood, Moss
review: Aventus-adjacent fruity chypre from Maison Asrar with a polarising reception. Opens with a bright pineapple-blackcurrant-grapefruit bomb over pink pepper; carnation and lily of the valley add florality in the heart; leather-moss-ambergris lands the drydown in classic chypre territory. The 'light airy top meeting dark moss and leather base' is the distinctive DNA. Performance is batch-variable — some report 7–8 hours, others find the musk dominates early. Reportedly improves significantly with 1–3 months of maceration post-opening.

name: Egra Men
review: Community consensus puts Egra Men among the best Creed GIT dupes at its price point — a cool, fresh fougère blending the citrus-violet-lavender DNA of GIT with faint Cool Water vibes. Opens on clean bergamot and grapefruit fizz, transitions to an iris-violet heart, dries down to soft sandalwood and musk. Performance divides opinion: 8–10 hours on some skin, moderate-then-skin-scent after 4 hours in heat. At under $30, the risk-to-reward is exceptional.

name: Rafan pour Homme
review: Community consistently calls Rafan one of the most faithful Le Sel d'Issey alternatives — many can't distinguish the two in blind tests, calling it 95% of the original. Opens with a striking salty-ginger accord; seaweed-vetiver adds a green, mineral edge in the heart; cedar-oakmoss grounds the finish in clean wood. Longevity impresses for an aquatic, often outlasting the original's modest EdT. The consensus go-to dupe for the sea-salt-in-a-bottle experience at a fraction of the Issey price.

name: Le Sel d'Issey
review: Divisive but distinctive — Issey's salty marine take that polarises with its unusual character. Opens on a striking sea salt and ginger burst; seaweed-vetiver adds a green, briny edge that some find unique and others find synthetic; cedarwood and oakmoss anchor into clean woody skin. 'Trademark Issey moderate' performance — 5–6 hours, 2–3 hours of active projection. Those who connect with it call it the most wearable sea-spray scent ever made. Best in summer or coastal settings. EdP version extends longevity to 7–8 hours.

name: Summer in Dubai
base: Tonka Bean, Patchouli, Ambroxan, Amber, Musk
review: Anfar 1950's care-free aromatic aquatic that punches well above its price point. Grapefruit and pineapple open with sea-spray freshness over bergamot; cardamom and ginger add spice without heat; ambroxan and tonka extend the trail into a clean, warm drydown. Community praises performance as exceptional for the price — 6–8 hours with strong projection. Called 'an absolute belter' and a consistent compliment getter. A summer daily driver.

name: Anfar Zenit Nomad
review: Anfar 1950's Zenit Series entry — described by reviewers as 'legendary.' Lemon-pineapple-bergamot open with pink pepper brightness; birch and oud create a smoky, woody heart lifted by rosemary and white amber; vanilla, leather, and vetiver ground the drydown in a complex animalic trail. Community advice: 2 sprays maximum — this is potent. Performance reports are extraordinary, with some clocking 24+ hours. Exceptional value in the Anfar Zenit line; a full-day commitment in a bottle.

name: Armaf Odyssey Limoni
inspiration: LV Freshie DNA — Afternoon Swim / Pacific Chill adjacent (not a 1:1 clone)
review: Bright, realistic iced-tea-and-lemons freshie — an amalgamation of the LV freshie DNAs rather than one accurate clone. Punches well above its price for compliments on hot days, but longevity is the clear weakness, fading to a skin scent inside a couple of hours.
layering: Use as a lemon-tea topper over {Turathi Blue} or {Aquatica} to refresh a fading citrus opening.

name: Afnan Supremacy Not Only Intense
inspiration: Creed Aventus (earthy Tom Ford Ombre Leather drydown)
review: Extrait de parfum (30%+ oil) built on the Aventus fruity-chypre idea but not a 1:1 clone — the oakmoss-patchouli heart dries down closer to Ombre Leather's earthy leather. Rich, versatile, and a genuine single-signature contender at the price.
layering: Layer {Qaed Al Fursan} over the top to inject a juicy pineapple lift into the leathery drydown.

name: Luxe Street
review: Maison Alhambra's Luxe-line rendition of Bond No. 9 Lafayette Street — community pegs it around 80% for the money. Sweet apple over an ambroxan-vanilla-tonka base makes it an approachable, cosy fruity-oriental for cooler evenings.
flag: ⚠️ Performance unverified — update longevity/projection after first wear.

name: Legesi
inspiration: Chanel Egoiste Platinum / Bleu de Chanel (softened barbershop twist)
review: Heavily-inspired Platinum Egoiste woody-aromatic softened with a vintage-fougère barbershop drydown and a touch of BdC. Projects well the first two hours. Known quirk: the opening 15-45 min can read harsh and slightly metallic before it settles into the clean shave-soap base.

name: Cosmic Giardino
review: 80-90% Paradise Garden with its own twist — sweeter and smoother than the original, leaning into lavender-violet-geranium and a salty coconut-fig freshness over a soft woody-tonka base. A juicy, slightly green summer crowd-pleaser.
layering: Pairs with {Aquatica} to deepen the coconut into a fuller tropical-beach accord.

name: Mercedes-Benz Club Black
review: A genuinely well-regarded designer original (not a clone). Bright bergamot flashes then gives way to smoky incense and jasmine over a rich, lightly caramelised vanilla-benzoin base that surrounds rather than shouts. A polished night/date scent that smells far above its price.
layering: Layer under {Khamrah Qahwa} for a smoky incense-coffee-vanilla evening cloud.

name: Afnan Rare Reef
top: Orange, Mint, Citron, Grapefruit, Blackcurrant, Coriander
review: Inspired by LV Pacific Chill but taken sweeter, fruitier and more citrus-forward — bright, beachy and clean with an apricot-fig warmth in the base. Not an exact clone (Pacific Chill is smoother and more apricot-heavy); reviews split on the synthetic citrus edge but praise the value.
layering: Layer over {Aquatica} to push a brighter citrus sparkle over the creamy coconut base.
